course_subtitle: In this module, you will explore the transformative landscape of Open Rack v3 (ORv3) and its pivotal role in shaping the future of data centers.
course_description_html: In this module, you will explore the transformative landscape of Open Rack v3 (ORv3) and its pivotal role in shaping the future of data centers. You will gain insights into how ORv3 addresses the increasing demands of AI infrastructure, supports sustainability initiatives, and contributes to the ambitious vision of achieving 1 megawatt rack capacity. By engaging with key trends and concepts, you will be equipped with the knowledge to navigate the evolving challenges and opportunities within the data center ecosystem.

blocks.body_html: By the end of this module, you’ll be able to:

items_html: Explain how ORv3 supports the evolving demands of AI infrastructure and contributes to the mission of 1 megawatt rack capacity
Identify key trends shaping the future of data centers, including power density, sustainability, and emerging performance requirements
Summarize the core concepts from the ORv3 course and access resources to support continued learning and implementation

media.alt: Why HPR v3 slide showing AI power demand, HPR advantages, horizontal busbars, and a paired HPRv3 rack system

blocks.body_html: The future of AI and high-performance computing is reshaping how data centers are built. With explosive growth in model size, compute intensity, and deployment scale, infrastructure must evolve—fast. In this module, we explore how Open Rack Version 3 (ORv3) supports this future, including the bold vision of a 1 megawatt (1MW) rack. You'll discover how ORv3 is enabling the next generation of AI data centers through smarter power delivery, scalable architecture, and integrated cooling solutions.

blocks.label: CONTINUE
blocks.complete_hint: Complete the content above before moving on.

lessons.title: The AI Data Center 1 Megawatt Rack: ORv3’s Future

blocks.label: CONTINUE
blocks.complete_hint: Complete the content above before moving on.

blocks.body_html:
The Open Rack ORv3 standard isn’t just meeting today’s data center needs—it’s shaping the infrastructure of tomorrow from high-power racks (HPR) to 1MW racks to sidecar power racks for entire deployment pods.

As AI workloads intensify and environmental pressures rise, data centers are facing a convergence of challenges. ORv3’s modular design, high-power capability, and support for advanced cooling are making it the backbone for scalable, sustainable deployments.

AI's high-power demands and change to +/-400V, 800V, or more may further change the busbar beyond what you learned in the module on high-powered racks (HPR). Voltage changes also impact the IT gear such as servers. For example, were the busbar itself carry a higher DC voltage, then the server's internal intermediate bus converters must adapt from converting 48V today as they step down to 6V or 12V across the boards and lower voltages regulated in the chips.
Click each card to explore a core industry trend shaping ORv3’s future

back.description_html: AI workloads require 100kW+ racks. ORv3’s scalable busbar and grounding systems support this shift for high powered compute trays and accelerator-based host process modules.
Air cooling alone is no longer enough. ORv3 simplifies liquid loop integration through blind-mate interfaces.
ORv3 supports modular, repairable components that reduce e-waste and extend product lifespan.
A vendor-neutral spec encourages broader interoperability and easier global deployment.
As workloads scale, ORv3 positions itself as a backbone for 1MW-class deployments.

items_html: Server Designs and Management for AI - Workshop Recording

blocks.body_html: You’ve just completed an in-depth look at the Open Rack ORv3 standard—from its community-driven origins to its future-focused role supporting high-power AI workloads.
Click each card to explore a core industry trend shaping ORv3’s future

items.title: Module 1: History and Purpose of OCP and ORv3
items.description_html: The Open Compute Project (OCP) was founded to rethink data center design through open, efficient, and scalable hardware standards. ORv3 is the third-generation open rack standard, developed to promote interoperability, sustainability, and performance across hyperscale and enterprise environments.

items.title: Module 2: ORv3 Core Specifications (rack, power, cooling)
items.description_html: ORv3 introduces a 21” open rack design with shared busbar power delivery, advanced airflow management, and liquid cooling readiness. Its modular components and support for both OCP and legacy 19” equipment make it adaptable to a wide range of data center needs.

items.title: Module 3: Implementations and Case Studies (Meta, Google)
items.description_html: Real-world deployments by Meta and Google demonstrate how ORv3 supports scalable, efficient infrastructure across diverse operational contexts. The OCP Marketplace and Community Contributions showcase the ecosystem’s innovation and vendor-neutral approach.

items.title: Module 4: High Power Rack Extensions and Ecosystem
items.description_html: The High Power Rack (HPR) ecosystem builds on ORv3 with extended busbars, front-service manifolds, and liquid cooling to support AI workloads exceeding 30kW per rack. These enhancements enable safe, serviceable, and high-density compute environments ready for the future.

items.title: Module 5: Future Trends in AI, Sustainability, and Power
items.description_html: ORv3 is a foundational platform for the next generation of data centers, supporting the journey toward 1 megawatt racks through modular upgrades and advanced cooling. Key trends like sustainability, power scaling, and global standardization are driving its evolution.

blocks.body_html: New course expansion: Continue with Module 6 for the Open Rack Wide (ORW) expansion, followed by Module 7 for advanced ORW liquid cooling practice.

blocks.label: CONTINUE
blocks.complete_hint: Complete the content above before moving on.

blocks.body_html:
Open Rack Base Spec

The core specification for ORv3, detailing form factors, power delivery, and compatibility options. https://www.opencompute.org/documents/open-rack-base-specification-version-3-rev1-1-030524-pdf

ORv3 IT Gear Design Guide Rev. 02

Engineering guidance for IT equipment integration and cooling. https://www.opencompute.org/documents/ocp-orv3-it-gear-design-guide-rev-02-june032024-pdf

Meta Implementation

The core specification for ORv3, detailing form factors, power delivery, and compatibility. https://www.opencompute.org/documents/open-rack-version-meta-v3-rev1pt3-june032024-pdf


Google Implementation

Practical deployment and specification adaptations for hyperscale environments. https://www.opencompute.org/documents/google-implementation-orv3-spec-1-pdf

Diablo 400 Power Rack

Sidecar power rack co-developed at OCP by Microsoft, Meta and Google.
https://www.opencompute.org/documents/ocp-specification-diablo-400-v0p5p2-2025-05-30-pdf

blocks.body_html: Test your knowledge from this module. Select the best answer for each question, then submit your answer.

blocks.question_html: What is one of the key design goals of the 1MW rack vision?
blocks.feedback_correct_html: Correct! 1MW racks aim to support high-density AI workloads.
blocks.feedback_incorrect_html: Not quite. 1MW racks aim to support high-density AI workloads.

blocks.label: CONTINUE
blocks.complete_hint: Complete the content above before moving on.

blocks.question_html: True or False? ORv3’s modular architecture is incompatible with future AI infrastructure needs.
blocks.feedback_correct_html: Correct! ORv3 is modular and designed to adapt to future needs.
blocks.feedback_incorrect_html: Not quite. ORv3 is modular and designed to adapt to future needs.

blocks.label: CONTINUE
blocks.complete_hint: Complete the content above before moving on.

blocks.question_html: The [answer] Rack Ecosystem is Meta’s OCP Community design for power scaling beyond 100kW per rack.
blocks.feedback_correct_html: Correct! The High Power Rack (HPR) Ecosystem supports >100kW per rack.
blocks.feedback_incorrect_html: Not quite. The High Power Rack (HPR) Ecosystem supports >100kW per rack.

blocks.label: CONTINUE
blocks.complete_hint: Complete the content above before moving on.

blocks.question_html: You are designing an AI cluster that may require up to hundreds of kW of rack-level capacity. What ORv3 adaptation should you consider?
blocks.feedback_correct_html: Correct! HPR busbar extensions support higher rack-level power capacity.
blocks.feedback_incorrect_html: Not quite. HPR busbar extensions support higher rack-level power capacity.
